textEn: Nūn. By the pen and what they [i.e., the angels] write [in the Records of deeds],
By the grace of your Lord [O Prophet], you are not a madman.
You will surely have a never-ending reward.
textTranslit: Wa inna laka la ajran ghaira mamnoon

textEn: Indeed, you are of a great moral character.
Soon you will see and they will see,
which of you is afflicted with madness.

textAr: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ
textEn: Indeed, your Lord alone knows best who has strayed from His way and those who are rightly guided.
textTranslit: Innaa Rabbaka Huwa a'lamu biman dalla 'an sabeelihee wa Huwa a'lamu bilmuhtadeen

textEn: So do not obey the deniers.
They wish that you would compromise so they would too compromise.
And do not obey every disgraceful swearer,
textTranslit: Wa laa tuti' kulla hallaa fim maheen

textEn: backbiter, gossip-monger,
withholder of good, transgressor, sinful,
coarse, and furthermore, an illegitimate child.
Simply because he has wealth and children,

textAr: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايَـٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
textEn: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, “Ancient fables!”
textTranslit: Izaa tutlaa 'alaihi aayaatunaa qaala asaateerul awwaleen

textEn: We will soon brand him on the snout.

textAr: إِنَّا بَلَوْنَـٰهُمْ كَمَا بَلَوْنَآ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا۟ لَيَصْرِمُنَّهَا مُصْبِحِينَ
textEn: Indeed, We have tested them [i.e., the Makkans] as We tested the people of the garden when they swore to harvest its fruits in the early morning,
textTranslit: Innaa balawnaahum kamaa balawnaaa As-haabal jannati iz 'aqsamoo la-yasri munnahaa musbiheen

textEn: and made no exception [by saying: if Allah wills].

textAr: فَطَافَ عَلَيْهَا طَآئِفٌ مِّن رَّبِّكَ وَهُمْ نَآئِمُونَ
textEn: Then it was struck by an affliction from your Lord while they were asleep,
textTranslit: Fataafa 'alaihaa taaa'i fum mir rabbika wa hum naaa'imoon

textEn: and it turned to ashes [as black as night].
Then in the early morning they called out to one another:

textAr: أَنِ ٱغْدُوا۟ عَلَىٰ حَرْثِكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰرِمِينَ
textEn: “Go early to your garden if you really wish to harvest.”
So they set out, whispering to one another,

textAr: أَن لَّا يَدْخُلَنَّهَا ٱلْيَوْمَ عَلَيْكُم مِّسْكِينٌ
textEn: “Not a single poor person should enter your garden today.”
And they left early, fully determined to stick to their plan.
But when they saw the garden, they said, “We must have lost our way!
textTranslit: Falammaa ra awhaa qaalooo innaa ladaaalloon

textEn: Rather, we have been deprived.”

textAr: قَالَ أَوْسَطُهُمْ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ لَوْلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ
textEn: The best among them said, “Did I not say to you to glorify Allah?”
textTranslit: Qaala awsatuhum alam aqul lakum law laa tusabbihoon

textAr: قَالُوا۟ سُبْحَـٰنَ رَبِّنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَـٰلِمِينَ
textEn: They said, “Glory be to our Lord! We were truly wrongdoers.”
textTranslit: Qaaloo subhaana rabbinaaa innaa kunnaa zaalimeen

textEn: Then they turned to one another, throwing blame.
They said, “Woe to us! We were surely transgressors.
textTranslit: Qaaloo yaa wailanaaa innaa kunnaa taagheen

textAr: عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَنَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهَآ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رَٰغِبُونَ
textEn: Our Lord may give us something better than this. We certainly turn to our Lord in hope.”
textTranslit: 'Asaa rabbunaaa any yubdilanaa khairam minhaaa innaaa ilaa rabbinaa raaghiboon

textAr: كَذَٰلِكَ ٱلْعَذَابُ ۖ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَكْبَرُ ۚ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ
textEn: Such is the punishment [in this world]; but the punishment of the Hereafter is far worse, if only they knew.
textTranslit: Kazaalikal azaab, wa la'azaabul aakhirati akbar; law kaanoo ya'lamoon

textAr: إِنَّ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّـٰتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ
textEn: Indeed, the righteous will have gardens of bliss with their Lord.
Should We then treat Muslims like the wicked?
What is the matter with you that you make such a judgment?
Or do you have a scripture in which you read
that you will be given whatever you choose?

textAr: أَمْ لَكُمْ أَيْمَـٰنٌ عَلَيْنَا بَـٰلِغَةٌ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۙ إِنَّ لَكُمْ لَمَا تَحْكُمُونَ
textEn: Or do you have a solemn promise binding upon Us until the Day of Resurrection, that you will have whatever you decide for yourselves?
textTranslit: Am lakum aymaanun 'alainaa baalighatun ilaa yawmil qiyaamati inna lakum lamaa tahkumoon

textEn: Ask them which of them can guarantee that.

textAr: أَمْ لَهُمْ شُرَكَآءُ فَلْيَأْتُوا۟ بِشُرَكَآئِهِمْ إِن كَانُوا۟ صَـٰدِقِينَ
textEn: Or do they have associate-gods? Then let them bring forth their associate-gods, if they are truthful.
textTranslit: Am lahum shurakaaa'u falyaatoo bishurakaaa 'ihim in kaanoo saadiqeen

textAr: يَوْمَ يُكْشَفُ عَن سَاقٍ وَيُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ
textEn: On the Day when the Shin [of Allah] will be uncovered, and they are invited to prostrate, but they will not be able to do so.
textTranslit: Yawma yukshafu 'am saaqinw wa yud'awna ilas sujoodi falaa yastatee'oon

textAr: خَـٰشِعَةً أَبْصَـٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ۖ وَقَدْ كَانُوا۟ يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سَـٰلِمُونَ
textEn: Their eyes will be downcast, overwhelmed with humiliation, for they were invited to prostrate when they were capable [but they refused].
textTranslit: Khaashi'atan absaaruhum tarhaquhum zillatunw wa qad kaanoo yud'awna ilassujoodi wa hum saalimoon

textAr: فَذَرْنِى وَمَن يُكَذِّبُ بِهَـٰذَا ٱلْحَدِيثِ ۖ سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُم مِّنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
textEn: So leave to Me those who reject this message [O Prophet]. We will gradually lead them to their ruin in ways that they do not perceive.
textTranslit: Fazarnee wa many yukazzibu bihaazal hadeesi sanastad rijuhum min haisu laa ya'lamoon

textEn: I will give them respite, but My plan is firm.
textTranslit: Wa umlee lahum; inna kaidee mateen

textAr: أَمْ تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ أَجْرًا فَهُم مِّن مَّغْرَمٍ مُّثْقَلُونَ
textEn: Or are you asking them for a reward, so they find it too burdensome?
textTranslit: Am tas'aluhum ajran fahum mim maghramim musqaloon

textEn: Or do they have knowledge of the unseen, so they write it down?

textAr: فَٱصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ ٱلْحُوتِ إِذْ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكْظُومٌ
textEn: So be patient with the decree of your Lord, and do not be like the man of the Whale [i.e., Jonah], when he cried out in anguish.
textTranslit: Fasbir lihkmi rabbika wa laa takun kasaahibil boot; iz naadaa wa huwa makzoom

textAr: لَّوْلَآ أَن تَدَٰرَكَهُۥ نِعْمَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ لَنُبِذَ بِٱلْعَرَآءِ وَهُوَ مَذْمُومٌ
textEn: Had it not been for the grace that he received from his Lord, he would have surely been thrown onto the barren shore, blameworthy.
textTranslit: Law laaa an tadaara kahoo ni'matum mir rabbihee lanubiza bil'araaa'i wa huwa mazmoom

textEn: But his Lord chose him and made him one of the righteous.

textAr: وَإِن يَكَادُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَيُزْلِقُونَكَ بِأَبْصَـٰرِهِمْ لَمَّا سَمِعُوا۟ ٱلذِّكْرَ وَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُۥ لَمَجْنُونٌ
textEn: The disbelievers would almost strike you down with their malicious gazes when they hear the Reminder, and they say, “He is certainly mad.”
textTranslit: Wa iny-yakaadul lazeena kafaroo la-yuzliqoonaka biabsaarihim lammaa saml'uz-Zikra wa yaqooloona innahoo lamajnoon

textEn: But it is nothing other than a reminder to the whole world.
textTranslit: Wa maa huwa illaa zikrul lil'aalameen
